summary: P3 Features Testing Complete - All 9 tests passed. Verified traffic-aware routing (time-of-day multipliers), cache stats with osrm_distance, and OSRM integration.

tests.name: GET /api/traffic/info returns current hour, multiplier, and schedule
tests.detail: Returns current_hour_utc, current_multiplier, and schedule with 8 time periods (night_free_flow, early_morning, am_peak, post_am_peak, midday, school_run, pm_peak, post_pm_peak)

tests.name: GET /api/cache/stats includes osrm_distance cache stats
tests.detail: Returns osrm_matrix, osrm_distance, and directions cache stats with entries, maxsize, ttl_seconds, hits, misses, hit_rate

tests.name: GET /api/directions returns source='osrm'
tests.detail: Returns source='osrm' with geometry (LineString), distance, duration, and steps

tests.name: POST /api/optimize with traffic_aware=true and departure_hour=8
tests.detail: Returns reasoning with 'traffic=1.35x@8:00' (AM peak multiplier applied)

tests.name: POST /api/optimize with traffic_aware=true and departure_hour=22
tests.detail: Returns reasoning with 'traffic=1.00x@22:00' (night free flow multiplier applied)

tests.name: POST /api/optimize without traffic_aware flag
tests.detail: Returns reasoning without traffic multiplier info when traffic_aware=False

tests.name: POST /api/optimize with traffic_aware=true and departure_hour=17
tests.detail: Returns reasoning with 'traffic=1.40x@17:00' (PM peak multiplier applied)

tests.name: POST /api/optimize returns valid results with OSRM
tests.detail: Returns valid optimization with OSRM duration matrix

critical_code_review_comments: _traffic_multiplier() correctly returns time-of-day multipliers: 1.35x (AM peak 7-9), 1.40x (PM peak 16-18), 1.20x (school run 15-16), 1.15x (post-peak), 1.05x (midday), 1.10x (early morning), 1.00x (night)
apply_traffic_multiplier() correctly scales duration matrix by multiplier, returns original if multiplier is 1.0
/api/traffic/info endpoint returns comprehensive schedule with all 8 time periods
/api/cache/stats now includes osrm_distance cache alongside osrm_matrix and directions caches
Traffic-aware optimization correctly logs multiplier application in backend logs
Reasoning string includes traffic info format: 'traffic=X.XXx@HH:00' when traffic_aware=True
test_credentials: DEV_MODE=True uses dev-user-123, no credentials needed
seed_data_creation: Test stops created/cleaned up via pytest fixtures (TEST_ prefixed)
context_for_next_testing_agent: P3 traffic-aware routing features are fully functional. Traffic multipliers: AM peak (7-9)=1.35x, PM peak (16-18)=1.40x, school run (15-16)=1.20x, post-peak=1.15x, midday=1.05x, early morning=1.10x, night=1.00x. Cache stats endpoint now returns 3 caches: osrm_matrix, osrm_distance, directions. OSRM runs on localhost:5000 with Queensland OSM data.

key_findings.traffic_info_endpoint: Working - returns current_hour_utc, current_multiplier, and full schedule
key_findings.cache_stats: Now includes osrm_matrix, osrm_distance, and directions caches
key_findings.traffic_aware_optimization: Correctly applies multiplier to duration matrix and includes in reasoning
key_findings.osrm_integration: Working - directions return source='osrm' with geometry and steps

mocked_apis_list: Authentication is mocked via DEV_MODE env var (dev-user-123)
